common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
auth.signInDesc: Choose how you'd like to sign in to access the Dashboard, Console, and infrastructure tools.
auth.emailMenuDesc: Choose how you'd like to sign in via email.
auth.emailOtpHint: You'll get a one-time code to access.
auth.emailMagicHint: We'll send you a sign-in link to access.
auth.emailOtpDesc: Enter your email and we'll send you a one-time code to sign in — no password needed.
auth.emailMagicDesc: Enter your email and we'll send you a secure sign-in link.
auth.emailMagicSignInHint: You can also click the sign-in link in the email.
auth.emailPasswordHint: Use your email and password to sign in.
auth.linkSentTo: We sent a sign-in link to {email}. Check your inbox.
auth.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters.
auth.passwordVerifyEmailBody: We sent a confirmation link to {email}. Open that email to finish creating your account.
auth.passwordSignInHint: Use your email and password to continue.
auth.passwordSignUpHint: Choose a strong password for your email login.
auth.restoringSessionDesc: Finishing secure sign-in before rendering the landing.
auth.signingOutDesc: Closing the Authentik session and returning you to the landing.
auth.otpSentTo: We sent a 6-digit code to {email}. Enter it below.
auth.resendError: Failed to resend code. Please try again.
auth.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
auth.otpSendError: Failed to send code. Please check the email address and try again.
auth.otpVerifyError: Invalid or expired code. Please try again.
auth.onTheList: You're on the list! We'll be in touch soon.
auth.cliDesc: Authenticate from your terminal. Run this command and follow the prompts to link your local machine.
auth.cliStep1: Run the command — it generates a one-time device code
auth.cliStep2: A browser window opens to confirm the code
auth.cliStep3: Sign in with any method (Google, email, etc.)
auth.cliStep4: Your CLI session is authenticated & token is cached locally
auth.sshDesc: Register your SSH public key to authenticate with CIG infrastructure services — ideal for automated pipelines and local development.
auth.sshAfter: After registering, you can push configs and access CIG services without a browser session.
legal.privacy.title: Privacy
legal.privacy.description: How CIG handles data across the landing site and dashboard.
legal.privacy.sections.collect.title: What we collect
legal.privacy.sections.collect.body: We keep data collection minimal. We only store information needed to authenticate users, operate the product, and keep the platform secure.
legal.privacy.sections.use.title: How we use it
legal.privacy.sections.use.body: We use data to sign users in, render the dashboard, remember preferences, and improve reliability and safety.
legal.privacy.sections.share.title: Sharing and retention
legal.privacy.sections.share.body: We do not sell personal data. We only share data with the services required to run CIG and retain it only as long as needed.
legal.privacy.note: If you have a privacy question, open an issue or contact the team through the deployment channel.
legal.terms.title: Terms
legal.terms.sections.access.title: Access
legal.terms.sections.access.body: Use the product only with accounts and environments you are authorized to manage.
legal.terms.sections.use.title: Acceptable use
legal.terms.sections.use.body: Do not use CIG to compromise systems, bypass controls, or violate applicable laws and policies.
legal.terms.sections.changes.title: Changes
legal.terms.sections.changes.body: We may update these terms when the product or its legal requirements change. Continued use means you accept the updated terms.
legal.terms.note: These pages are intentionally minimal. Please review the source repository or deployment notes for the current legal owner.
